ranked_movies.title: Burning
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed and highly recommended across multiple posts. Praised as a tense, well-constructed thriller with an open-ended twist, and frequently compared to 'Parasite' and 'The Handmaiden'. Considered the most accessible of Lee Chang-dong's films.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as the original South Korean film that 'Zinda' is an unauthorized adaptation of. The discussion around 'Zinda' inherently brings attention to 'Oldboy' as a classic and superior film.

ranked_movies.title: Peppermint Candy
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended as a heartbreaking masterpiece that chronicles a man's descent alongside modern South Korean history. Described as emotionally impactful and groundbreaking.

ranked_movies.title: Oasis
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as an uncomfortable yet powerful love story that raises important points about the treatment of people with disabilities. Noted for its strong performances and emotional weight.

ranked_movies.title: Secret Sunshine
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a heavy and well-done examination of grief and religion. Praised for its exploration of deep themes and strong acting.

ranked_movies.title: Poetry
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned in the comments as a must-see Lee Chang-dong film, with some users calling it his masterpiece and better than 'Burning'. Described as a film about empathy.

ranked_movies.title: The Negotiation
ranked_movies.reason: Promoted across multiple subreddits as a 'Best Korean Thriller Movie' starring Hyun Bin and Son Ye-jin. The repeated promotional posts increase its visibility and discussion.

Specifically recommended as one of the best queer films ever made in Korea. The post provides a detailed and passionate recommendation, highlighting its realistic depiction of Korean gay culture and its status as a coming-of-age story.

ranked_movies.title: Zinda
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as an unauthorized Bollywood adaptation of 'Oldboy'. The conversation, while mixed, brings attention to the film's soundtrack and performances, creating a notable discussion point.

ranked_movies.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey
ranked_movies.reason: Analyzed for its potential box office performance. The post discusses its high-profile cast (Margot Robbie, Colin Farrell), its director, and its acquisition cost, generating speculative discussion about its commercial prospects.

ranked_movies.title: After Yang
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as the previous film by director Kogonada, which 'made nothing' at the box office. This reference provides context for the discussion around his new film, 'A Big Bold Beautiful Journey'.

ranked_movies.title: Green Fish
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in comments as part of Lee Chang-dong's filmography that should be watched to complete it. Its mention, though brief, adds it to the list of discussed films.

ranked_movies.title: The Handmaiden
ranked_movies.reason: Used as a point of comparison for 'Burning', indicating it is a well-known and respected film within the community discussing South Korean cinema.

ranked_movies.title: Parasite
ranked_movies.reason: Used as a point of comparison for 'Burning', solidifying its status as a benchmark for high-quality, critically regarded South Korean thrillers.

ranked_movies.title: Mist Whispers Like a Woman
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the historical context of the unresolved mystery post as one of the films actress Yoon Young-sil starred in. This tangential mention places it in the discussion.

ranked_movies.title: What will we become and where will we meet again
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the historical context of the unresolved mystery post as one of the films actress Yoon Young-sil starred in. This tangential mention places it in the discussion.

ranked_movies.title: The Menu
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a previous work by the screenwriter of 'A Big Bold Beautiful Journey', Seth Reiss. This connection is used to gauge the potential quality of the new film.

ranked_movies.title: Shootout at Lokhandwala
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in a comment about the 'Zinda' soundtrack, as the composers also contributed a song to this film. This tangential connection brings it into the discussion.

ranked_movies.title: Josee, the Tiger and the Fish
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in a negative, argumentative comment comparing 'Oasis' to it, claiming 'Oasis' is a ripoff. This controversial mention adds it to the list of discussed films.

ranked_movies.title: Whispering Corridors 6
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in a comment as a film that serendipitously provided context for understanding 'Peppermint Candy'. This unique, personal connection gets it a mention.
